betterquesting:10.desc:8:
The attitude of my past enemies towards Alchemy is a strange one. 
Like a jealous kid seeing another kid playing with afun toy, and trying to steal it, not to take it for themselves, but to destroy it. "If I cannot enjoy it, nobody can enjoy it!"
Not just with respect to Alchemy, but with the power of the Bees. Their Queen, the one kidnapped long ago, hidden behind a strange prison of recursion, Space, and Will.
The act of the kidnapping did not lead to any meaningful advantage to the Shyre Empire nor the Twilight Horde. They siply took away theoretical power from a peaceful people who was even willing to help them.
I guess this is what power does to you. I get it. 
It feels special at first, and then it fades, it becomes less important, less emaningful. Nothing will ever compare to the feeling of coming out of Vethea, and even now I find myself chasing it, in a way.
Werdly enough, I feel like the temptations of the Furnace Dimension acted as a sort of training for the hunger I feel now. The hunger to chase more power, to claim more from recursion.
